package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for transaction_object_crosses_more_than_one_layer_boundary: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Transaction handles passed through multiple layers as ordinary arguments.
type CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryModel
}

type CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryService struct {
    repo *CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundary(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryDTO) error {
    focus := "transaction_object_crosses_more_than"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    tx := db.Begin()
    tx.Create(&CaseTransactionObjectCrossesMoreThanOneLayerBoundaryModel{ID: input})
    go func() { _ = tx.Commit().Error }()
    transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive := map[string]int{"transaction": 1, "object": 2, "crosses": 3, "more": 4, "than": 5, "one": 6}
    transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    transaction_positive_0 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["transaction"] + len(input)
    _ = transaction_positive_0
    object_positive_1 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["object"] + len(input)
    _ = object_positive_1
    crosses_positive_2 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["crosses"] + len(input)
    _ = crosses_positive_2
    more_positive_3 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["more"] + len(input)
    _ = more_positive_3
    than_positive_4 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["than"] + len(input)
    _ = than_positive_4
    one_positive_5 := transaction_object_crosses_more_than_positive["one"] + len(input)
    _ = one_positive_5
    return nil
}
